titleStmt.title: SII 37.511: original edition by S. Nagarjuna and K. Munirathnam
No. 511. (A.R. No. B 11 of 1943-44.) Nandavaram, Banganapalle Taluk, Kurnool District.

summary.p: Slab paved on the floor on the north side of prākāra of the Chauḍēśvari temple.
This undated inscription may be assigned to the 17th-18th century, on the basis of palaeography.
It states that Apparāju, son of Teḷḷapūḍi Chiṭṭamarāju worshipped the goddess Chāmuṇḍēśvari to ward off all evils and attain bliss.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
tēḷlapūḍi ciṭṭamarāju komāruḍu Apparāju yidivaraku tana jaṁnmajaṁnmāṁttarālu
yeṁnnigalavō Anniṁṭiyaṁddu cēśina sakala pāpāluṁnnu vimōcanamai sūryuḍu moda
laina navagrahāluṁnnu vedhalumāni śubhamēkādaśaḥsthānamaṁdduvuṁnna phalaṁbule yalla
ppaṭiki yiccucū duḥkhaṁbulu parihāraṁbai gaṁḍaṁbulu vāydholgi vighnaṁbulu daṭṭaka
sakala kāryaṁbulu saphalaṁbai sukhaṁbulu śidhiṁbeṭaṭṭugānu mā yiṣṭavēlpu mahāśakti
kōṭi sūryulabhaṁgi divyatējōmayaṁbaina paraṁjyōtiyagu yī naṁdavaramu cāmuṁḍī
mahādēviki sārvakālālayaṁdduṁnnu Akhaṁḍa daṁḍāyamānaṁgā tana hṛdaya
kamala kaṁrṇnikā madhyamaṁddu sakalōpacārālatōnu pārijātādulu modalaina
baṁgāru puṣpālatō pūjacēstā dēvidhyāna paravaśuḍai lalitāsahasranāma maṁtra
japaṁ cēstā nityānaṁddābhyudayuḍai Aṁmmavāri sāṁnidhyamaṁdu Abhayuḍaivuṁnnāḍu
Digital edition of SII 37.511 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
